title: Rotorua Geothermal Parks Guide — Te Puia vs Wai-O-Tapu vs Hell's Gate
meta_desc: Discover the best Rotorua thermal parks for families, comparing Te Puia, Wai-O-Tapu, and Hell's Gate
intro: Rotorua is a hotspot for geothermal activity, with three main parks to choose from: Te Puia, Wai-O-Tapu, and Hell's Gate. Each offers a unique experience, but which one is best for your family? With prices ranging from $30 to $80 per person, it's essential to make an informed decision. In this guide, we'll compare the three parks to help you plan your trip.

sections.heading: Overview of Rotorua Geothermal Parks
sections.body: Te Puia, Wai-O-Tapu, and Hell's Gate are the three main geothermal parks in Rotorua, each offering a distinct experience. Te Puia is known for its cultural performances and kiwi bird sanctuary, while Wai-O-Tapu is famous for its colorful hot springs and geysers. Hell's Gate, on the other hand, is a more rugged and natural park with a mud spa and hot waterfalls.

sections.heading: Best for Families
list: Te Puia: suitable for families with children of all ages, with a kiwi bird sanctuary and cultural performances
Wai-O-Tapu: suitable for families with children aged 5 and above, with colorful hot springs and geysers
Hell's Gate: suitable for families with children aged 8 and above, with a mud spa and hot waterfalls

sections.heading: Getting Around Rotorua
sections.body: The drive times between the geothermal parks and Rotorua CBD are relatively short, with Te Puia being the closest. Wai-O-Tapu is about 20 minutes away, while Hell's Gate is 15 minutes away. You can rent a car or take a shuttle bus to get to the parks. A campervan is also a great option, allowing you to travel at your own pace and stay overnight in the area.

sections.heading: Tips and Essentials
sections.body: Remember to wear comfortable shoes and bring sunscreen, as well as a camera to capture the stunning scenery. It's also a good idea to check the weather forecast before heading out, as the parks can be closed due to bad weather. With prices starting from $30 per person, it's essential to plan ahead and make the most of your visit.
